paper_title: The Trans -2/9 Identity for Degree-(2,1) Polynomial Continued Fractions: Empirical Evidence, an Algebraic Signature, and the Limits of Standard Asymptotic Methods
key_theorems: Theorem 3.1 plus-form indicial pair {1/3,2/3} on r=-2/9 (trivial restatement)
Theorem 4.3 algebraic signature: a2/b1^2 = -2/9 iff |mu+/mu-| = (13+3sqrt17)/4 (sign condition a2<0)
Theorem 4.4 PSLQ certificate [13,3,-4] at dps=150, phantom-hit-rule passes
Theorem 5.3 integer-grid obstruction (W1 negative result): no integer family realises minus-form indicial {1/3,2/3} and r=-2/9 simultaneously
Proposition 5.4 leading-order Birkhoff invariant search (W2 negative result)
Proposition 5.5 + Corollary 5.6 sub-leading c1 coefficient (W3 negative result): A_num = (a2-b1mu)(4a2-b1^2), vanishes only on Alg discriminant-zero locus
Propositions 5.6-5.7 convergence-rate and direct-PSLQ negative results

compilation.warnings: hyperref non-Unicode token warnings (cosmetic, in PDF bookmarks); one Unicode Gamma in source fixed before final compile

length_note: Compiled to 14 pages, below the 18-25 target. Density is high (compact theorems + tables); a future revision could expand the W1/W2/W3 derivations inline rather than citing the sprint reports, plus add an explicit Schwarz-list/Galois discussion section, to reach 18-25.
